# Fertilizer Programme

**Reference Block — Macadamia — Levubu Reference**
*Levubu, Limpopo*
**Macadamia · 2026/27**  
*Planting date: 01 March 2026*
*Reference: REF-MAC-2026*

---

## Background

This programme covers **Macadamia** for **2026/27**, spanning 1 block across 5.00 ha. The strategy is built around dry broadcast / basal applications, fertigation through the irrigation system, and targeted foliar corrections, sized against the soil analysis and the expected harvest.

The sections that follow set out what the soil is telling us, how the programme addresses the priorities, the applications scheduled through the season, and the open items worth tracking.

---

## Reading of the Soil

Soil analysis for **Reference Block A4 (Levubu)** (5.00 ha) was reviewed against the crop's nutrient requirements for the season.

*Sample: Reference block (published norms) · Mar 2026*

**What the soil is flagging:**
- Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds 1000:1 — Ca-induced B deficiency likely, especially at flowering.
- pH (KCl) 4.80 below crop-optimal band (5.0–6.5). Acidic soil restricts Ca / Mg / P availability; root expansion limited.

---

## The Strategy

The programme serves the following objectives:

**(a)** Carry the crop through the season with moderate nitrogen, adequate phosphorus and potassium — drawing on what's already in the soil and the products on hand.

**(b)** Anchor each dry blend with the organic carrier at a minimum of 50 % of blend mass — delivering organic carbon, slow-release nitrogen, and the full micronutrient package while building soil for next season and beyond.

**(c)** Deliver stage-peak nutrition through the irrigation system in measured doses, matching the crop's uptake curve rather than front-loading the season.

**(d)** Use targeted foliar applications to bypass soil lockup or correct stage-peak deficiencies that soil-delivered nutrients can't meet fast enough.

---

**Foliar sprays**

| # | Week | Stage | Analysis | Rate | Reason |
|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| #1 | 6 | Vegetative I | 20.5% B | 1.5 kg | Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds 1000:1 — Ca-induced B deficiency likely, especially at flowering. |
| #2 | 28 | Pre-flowering | 20.5% B | 0.1% spray | Critical for flower set + nut formation; B mobile in xylem only |
| #3 | 32 | Vegetative flush | 36% Zn | 0.5% spray | Leaf expansion + enzyme activation; soil Zn often unavailable |

---

## Why the Programme Works Over Multiple Years

The organic carrier at the heart of every dry blend is doing three different jobs on three different timescales:

**Inside the first month** — soluble nutrients + available organic fraction release fast, covering the immediate crop demand. Calcium and sulphur from the carrier support cell-wall integrity and root function through establishment.

**Inside the first season** — roughly 20–30 % of the carrier's nitrogen mineralises and becomes plant-available, along with the full micronutrient package (boron, zinc, manganese, copper). This is why separate boron or micro broadcasts are usually unnecessary when the carrier is doing the work.

**Over the next 2–4 years** — the remaining 70 % of nitrogen releases gradually as soil biology mineralises the organic fraction. Carbon accumulates: this programme delivers roughly 75 kg organic C per hectare this season. On OM-poor SA soils that's measurable lift in next year's soil test, and it compounds when the programme is repeated annually.

For a macadamia stand this matters especially: perennial root systems depend on soil structure and biology as much as on chemistry. The long-run programme is building the field the crop lives in, not just feeding this season's growth.

---

| Nutrient | kg / ha |
|---|---:|
| N | 145.0 |
| P2O5 | 60.0 |
| K2O | 260.0 |
| Ca | 0.0 |
| Mg | 0.0 |
| S | 22.0 |

---

## Ratios — Where You Are

These are the nutrient and soil ratios that shape how the programme is sized. Where you are today, and what the target band looks like.

| Metric | Value | Target band |
|---|---:|---|
| Sodium saturation (ESP %) | 1.0 % | Below 5 % is clean; 10–15 % is trending sodic; above 15 % needs active gypsum work. |
| P : Zn | 7.3 : 1 | Below ~100 : 1 is comfortable; above 150 : 1 restricts Zn uptake regardless of soil Zn. |
| Ca : B | 2800 : 1 | Target below ~1000 : 1 for most crops; excess Ca locks B away from uptake. |

---

## Year 2 and Beyond

This is a perennial macadamia programme, so Year 1 is the establishment or maintenance baseline — the real story is what the stand looks like after a few seasons on the same approach.

**Three-year targets to track:**

- **Soil organic matter** lifting toward 2 % (or higher for orchards). Measurable on next year's soil test.
- **Soil pH** moving toward the crop's preferred band. This is a multi-season lift; year-on-year re-test tracks progress.
- **Phosphorus** building from Low toward Optimal on soil test.
- **Boron** in the crop-optimal band (varies by crop; typical 1.0–2.5 mg/kg).

**The ongoing programme to get there is straightforward:** continue the carrier + targeted synthetic approach at similar cadence annually, and top up phosphorus, potassium, and any flagged limiting nutrients each season against real post-harvest soil data rather than speculation.

---

## Items To Track

### Risk flags

- **[WARN]** Acidic pH but no time to lime (gap -1.8 months, lime needs 3-6). Ca / Mg / P availability will run below capacity this season; lean on foliar Ca + Mg at critical stages and avoid acidifying N sources.
- **[WARN]** Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds 1000:1 — Ca-induced B deficiency likely, especially at flowering. — Action: Apply foliar B at critical stages (pre-bloom, fruit set)
- **[WARN]** pH (KCl) 4.80 below crop-optimal band (5.0–6.5). Acidic soil restricts Ca / Mg / P availability; root expansion limited. — Action: Lime application (calcitic or dolomitic per Mg status). Size against pH deficit + buffer factor; pre-season for reaction time.

### Outstanding items

- **Lime application — too late this season for full reaction** — Soil acidity / Al saturation needs lime to correct. Lime requires 3-6 months to react fully. Build-to-plant gap is only -1.8 months — insufficient for this season. Schedule lime for post-harvest of this crop to set up the next season. — *Impact if skipped: Acidity / Al stress persists into the following season; early-growth and P availability will under-perform*
- **Irrigation water test (EC, pH, Ca, Mg, Na, HCO₃, Cl)** — Required before first Part A injection. Dolomite-country water can produce scale when calcium-stream stock is injected. Borehole water EC + bicarb level affect drip-line pH. — *Impact if skipped: Risk of emitter blockage from CaCO₃ precipitate*
- **Mid-season leaf tissue analysis at tillering and at bulb-init/flowering** — Two natural decision points where the programme can still be steered. Catches deficiencies the soil test missed and validates actual uptake against plan. — *Impact if skipped: Mid-season corrections become reactive, not anticipatory*
